name: Long Method
pattern: function body exceeding 50 lines
suggestion: Break into smaller, focused methods with single responsibility

name: God Class
pattern: class with more than 20 methods or 500 lines
suggestion: Split into smaller classes using SRP principle

name: Magic Numbers
pattern: hardcoded numeric literals in logic
suggestion: Extract to named constants with descriptive names

name: Deep Nesting
pattern: code nested more than 3 levels deep
suggestion: Use early returns, guard clauses, or extract methods

name: Duplicate Code
pattern: repeated code blocks across methods
suggestion: Extract common logic into shared functions or utilities

name: Dead Code
pattern: unreachable or unused code blocks
suggestion: Remove dead code to reduce confusion and maintenance burden

name: Feature Envy
pattern: method accessing another class data extensively
suggestion: Move method to the class whose data it primarily uses

name: Data Clumps
pattern: groups of data frequently appearing together
suggestion: Group into a class or data structure

name: Primitive Obsession
pattern: overuse of primitives instead of domain objects
suggestion: Create domain-specific value objects

name: Long Parameter List
pattern: method with more than 4 parameters
suggestion: Use parameter objects or builder pattern

name: Switch Statements
pattern: complex switch/case blocks
suggestion: Replace with polymorphism or strategy pattern

name: Speculative Generality
pattern: unused abstractions or interfaces
suggestion: Remove unused abstractions; add them when actually needed

name: Temporary Field
pattern: object fields only set in certain circumstances
suggestion: Extract to separate class or use null object pattern

name: Message Chains
pattern: long chains of method calls a.b().c().d()
suggestion: Apply Law of Demeter; use delegate methods

name: Middle Man
pattern: class delegating all work to another class
suggestion: Remove middleman and call delegate directly

name: Inappropriate Intimacy
pattern: classes accessing each other private internals
suggestion: Move methods, extract class, or use proper interfaces

name: Refused Bequest
pattern: subclass not using inherited methods
suggestion: Use composition instead of inheritance

name: Comments as Deodorant
pattern: excessive comments explaining bad code
suggestion: Refactor code to be self-documenting

name: Inconsistent Naming
pattern: mixed naming conventions in same scope
suggestion: Adopt and enforce consistent naming conventions

name: Global State
pattern: mutable global variables shared across modules
suggestion: Use dependency injection or module-scoped state

name: Callback Hell
pattern: deeply nested callbacks
suggestion: Use Promises, async/await, or reactive patterns

name: Shotgun Surgery
pattern: single change requires editing many classes
suggestion: Consolidate related logic into a single module

name: Parallel Inheritance
pattern: subclassing one class requires subclassing another
suggestion: Merge hierarchies or use composition

name: Lazy Class
pattern: class that does too little to justify existence
suggestion: Inline class into its caller

name: Boolean Parameters
pattern: methods with boolean flags changing behavior
suggestion: Split into two separate methods

name: Mutable Default Arguments
pattern: mutable objects as default function parameters
suggestion: Use None/null as default and create new object inside function

name: String Typing
pattern: using strings where enums or types should be used
suggestion: Replace with enums, constants, or type unions

name: Circular Dependencies
pattern: modules importing each other
suggestion: Extract shared code to a third module

name: Overly Complex Regex
pattern: regex patterns that are hard to understand
suggestion: Break into named groups or use a parser library

name: Empty Catch Blocks
pattern: catching exceptions without handling them
suggestion: Log the error or handle it appropriately

name: Hardcoded File Paths
pattern: absolute file paths in source code
suggestion: Use configuration files or environment variables

name: Console.log in Production
pattern: debug logging left in production code
suggestion: Use a proper logging framework with log levels

name: Unused Imports
pattern: imported modules never referenced
suggestion: Remove unused imports

name: Mixed Abstraction Levels
pattern: method mixing high-level and low-level operations
suggestion: Separate into methods at consistent abstraction levels

name: Excessive Return Values
pattern: returning more than 3 values from a function
suggestion: Return an object or create a result class

name: Tight Coupling
pattern: classes directly depending on implementation details
suggestion: Program to interfaces, use dependency injection

name: Missing Error Handling
pattern: operations that can fail without try-catch or checks
suggestion: Add proper error handling and recovery logic

name: Nested Ternary
pattern: ternary operators within ternary operators
suggestion: Use if-else statements or extract to named variables

name: Oversized File
pattern: source file exceeding 500 lines
suggestion: Split into multiple focused modules

name: No Unit Tests
pattern: complex logic without corresponding tests
suggestion: Write unit tests covering critical paths and edge cases
